settings.title: Settings Center
settings.subtitle: Personalized settings for a better experience
settings.userAccountDesc: Click the avatar icon on the right to manage your account settings
settings.pushNotificationsDesc: Receive habit reminders and achievement notifications
settings.showProgressDesc: Display progress bars in reward cards
settings.exportSuccessDesc: Your data has been successfully exported to downloads folder
settings.importSuccessDesc: Your data has been successfully imported
settings.importFailedDesc: File format is incorrect, please check and try again
settings.dataClearedDesc: All data has been successfully cleared
settings.resetConfirm: Are you sure you want to reset to default settings? This will restore sample data.
settings.clearConfirm: Are you sure you want to clear all data? This action cannot be undone!
settings.resetSuccessDesc: App has been reset to default state, sample data has been restored
settings.dataBackupTip: The data export function will save all your habits, rewards and completion records as a JSON file. Regular backup is recommended.
settings.compatibilityLabDesc: Reward-first is now the default experience, while legacy modules stay available here during migration.
settings.compatibilityNoteDesc: The old habits, rewards, bindings, and history views were not removed. They are grouped into one compatibility workspace to avoid losing migration-time access.

vnextShell.description: Use action to charge the rewards you truly want, instead of managing a pile of modules.

dashboard.heroEmptyTitle: Create your first reward plan and start the flywheel
dashboard.heroEmptyDescription: Turn the reward you really want into a plan, then prove it deserves redemption through action.
dashboard.todayActionsHint: The most worthwhile actions for today, ranked by impact.
dashboard.activePlansHint: Includes active, validating, and ready-to-redeem plans.
dashboard.lifetimeEnergyHint: A long-term indicator of effort and growth.
dashboard.todayActionsDescription: Start with the work that best advances your current reward plan.
dashboard.noTodayActions: There are no actionable items yet. Once you create a reward plan, today's best actions will show up here automatically.
dashboard.advancesReward: Completing this directly advances “{{reward}}”; {{remaining}} more times remain
dashboard.unassignedPlan: Not assigned to a plan yet. Adopt it from the plan detail view.
dashboard.validationSignalsDescription: What desires have recently been validated, redeemed, or filtered out.
dashboard.topPlansDescription: Prioritize the goals closest to redemption.

plans.title: Plans
plans.description: Manage goals through reward plans, instead of splitting rewards and bindings across separate modules.
plans.emptyGroup: There are no plans in this state yet.

review.title: Review
review.description: This is not just statistics. It shows what you've really invested in lately, and which desires are still worth pursuing.

energyTrend.title: Energy trend
energyTrend.description: Use time to see how your recent effort density changes.

rankings.plansDescription: Which goals absorb most of your action and energy.
rankings.habitsDescription: Which actions truly move your goals.

summary.title: Validation summary
summary.description: Redeemed and abandoned desires together shape your judgment system.

validated.title: Rewards proven worthwhile
validated.description: Which goals have already been proven worth redeeming by action.
validated.empty: There are no redeemed plans yet.

filtered.title: Filtered desires
filtered.description: Not failure, but an effective clarification of desire.
filtered.empty: There are no abandoned plans right now.

library.title: Library
library.description: Archive completed, abandoned, and inactive items so the current execution layer stays clean.

archivedPlans.title: Completed / abandoned plans
archivedPlans.description: These outcomes are themselves records of desire validation.
archivedPlans.empty: There are no archived plans yet.

archivedHabits.title: Archived habits
archivedHabits.description: No longer part of the active flywheel, but kept as future templates or review evidence.

repeatableTemplates.title: Repeatable reward templates
repeatableTemplates.description: Clone already-validated repeatable rewards into the next cycle instead of configuring them from scratch.
repeatableTemplates.empty: There are no reusable repeatable reward templates yet.

habitTemplates.title: General habit templates
habitTemplates.description: Archiving is not deletion. Good action patterns should be easy to reuse.
habitTemplates.empty: There are no reusable habit templates yet.

recommendations.title: Recommended template slot
recommendations.description: Reserved for future curated templates and plan duplication entries; for now the focus stays on your own archive.
recommendations.placeholder: Next, this area can include system templates, community templates, or one-click plan duplication.

planComposer.rewardNamePlaceholder: Example: Upgrade to GPT Plus / Buy a push-up board
planComposer.descriptionPlaceholder: What is this reward, and what does redeeming it mean?
planComposer.motivationPlaceholder: Why is this worth pursuing? How do you know it isn't just an impulse?
planComposer.actionsDescription: You can create new actions or adopt existing habits into this plan.
planComposer.actionNamePlaceholder: Example: Use the full O3 conversation budget every week
planComposer.actionDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe the completion criteria so it doesn't become a vague check-in later.
planComposer.confirmDescription: Check whether the reward definition, status, and action mix make sense.
planComposer.dialogDescription: Define what you want first, then break it into executable actions.

planDetail.dialogDescription: Operate the reward as a plan, not as an ordinary redemption record.
planDetail.bindingDescription: Manage all bound actions here instead of jumping to a separate binding page.
planDetail.emptyBinding: No actions are bound yet. Break the reward into concrete actions before the flywheel can really spin.

redeemDialog.title: Redeem reward confirmation
redeemDialog.description: Redemption is not a trivial click. It formally settles this whole stretch of action.
redeemDialog.validatedWindow: From {{start}} to today, this reward has been proven by enough action.
redeemDialog.insufficientEnergy: Current energy is still not enough. Keep pushing before redeeming.

abandonDialog.title: Abandon plan
abandonDialog.description: Abandoning is not failure. It means confirming this reward is not worth more investment right now.
abandonDialog.reasonPlaceholder: Example: after trying it, the real need felt weak, interest shifted, or the goal changed
abandonDialog.reflection: What did you learn this time?
abandonDialog.reflectionPlaceholder: Record a useful desire filter instead of leaving only a failure label.

legacyWorkspace.title: Compatibility workspace
legacyWorkspace.description: vNext is now centered on reward plans, but the old habits, rewards, bindings, and history modules remain here for use during migration.

migrationAdvice.title: Migration advice
migrationAdvice.primaryFlow: Use `Dashboard / Plans / Review / Library` as the default primary flow.
migrationAdvice.quickEntry: If you only want to quickly add a habit or reward, you can still use the legacy forms.
migrationAdvice.compatibilityOnly: Legacy binding and legacy history are compatibility views only, and will gradually give way to plan detail and Review.

forms.habitEntryDescription: Direct habit creation still works and can still bind to existing rewards. This entry has been downgraded into the compatibility layer.
forms.rewardEntryDescription: Direct reward creation still works, but the recommended path is now to create a plan first and configure actions inside that plan.

legacyWorkspace.historyNotice: This is the compatibility mount point for the legacy history page. The main review experience has moved to `Review`, but the full old view still stays here.

bindingManager.title: Binding management
bindingManager.description: Bind habits to rewards so every effort has a clear target
bindingManager.emptyDescription: Create a binding so habits and rewards become connected.

toast.missingSelectionTitle: Choose a habit and a reward
toast.missingSelectionDescription: You need to select one habit and one reward to create a binding.
toast.removedDescription: The binding between “{{habit}}” and “{{reward}}” has been removed

historyView.title: History
historyView.description: Review your growth path and let the data witness your effort

range.weekDescription: View the last 7 days of data
range.monthDescription: View the last 30 days of data

historyView.emptyDescription: Start completing habits and your growth path will appear here

chart.title: Last 7 days trend
chart.legend: Solid line = completions, dashed line = gained energy

toast.thresholdReached: The redemption threshold has been reached. You can settle this reward now.
toast.planCreatedDescription: Created “{{name}}”. Let action charge it next.
toast.templateCopiedDescription: Created a new reward plan based on “{{name}}”.
toast.habitTemplateCopiedDescription: Generated a new active habit from “{{name}}”.
toast.planRedeemedDescription: This reward has now been formally settled. You can revisit its action trail later in Library.
toast.planAbandonedDescription: This was not failure, but a clear filtering of desire.

milestone.reachedDescription: This advance pushed the plan to {{current}}/{{target}} ⚡. You can move to redemption confirmation now.
milestone.sprintDescription: You've crossed the 80% mark. Only {{remaining}} ⚡ remain to complete this validation cycle.

defaultPlan.name: Example: Upgrade to GPT Plus
defaultPlan.description: Use real action to validate whether this tool is actually valuable to me.
defaultPlan.motivation: Use the current capability fully before deciding to upgrade.
defaultPlan.habitDescription: Concentrate the week's deepest questions and resolve them thoroughly.

welcome.title: Habit Flywheel: turn routines into a rewarding loop
welcome.subtitle: Connect habits, energy, and rewards so every check-in fuels the next action.

features.title: Why Habit Flywheel
features.subtitle: Bring clarity to your routines and get instant feedback from rewards.

flywheel.title: Flywheel workflow
flywheel.description: Plan habits, collect energy, and redeem rewards to build momentum.

rewards.title: Reward binding
rewards.description: Link meaningful rewards to habit completion and see the payoff immediately.

supabase.title: Secure with Supabase
supabase.description: Supabase auth and row level security keep your data private and synced.

pwa.title: Ready anywhere
pwa.description: Install as a PWA to use Habit Flywheel offline on desktop or mobile.

preview.title: Real-time energy snapshot
preview.description: A sample of energy gains and reward progress—your data updates with every check-in.
preview.bindingExample: Completing it adds 30 energy to the linked reward automatically.
preview.disclaimer: Preview data is illustrative; sign in to see your real dashboard.

cta.title: Ready to spin up your habit flywheel?
cta.subtitle: Create an account, connect habits with rewards, and start building momentum today.

story.title: How does the Habit Flywheel work?
story.subtitle: A repeatable system that links desire, habits, and the rewards you care about.

items.title: Validate the reward first
items.description: Treat every reward as a hypothesis. Run challenges and track check-ins to see if it truly motivates you before investing time and money.

items.title: Bind habits to the reward
items.description: Break the reward into daily habits and assign energy values so every action earns visible progress toward the payoff.

steps.title: Name the desire
steps.description: Capture the reward you crave and treat it as a demand to be validated.

steps.title: Design a challenge
steps.description: Time-box a challenge around it. Completion data tells you whether the reward is truly meaningful.

steps.title: Bind habits
steps.description: Split the challenge into repeatable habits, each with an energy value you earn once finished.

steps.title: Redeem the payoff
steps.description: When energy reaches the threshold, claim the reward and reinforce the loop.

narrative.title: Rooted in lived experience
paragraphs: The author struggled with study plans and productivity systems that never answered whether the reward was worth the effort.
The habit flywheel reframes the process as a loop of desire, validation, execution, and reinforcement so every action maps to a tangible benefit.
Use it as a personal operating system where rewards, habits, and energy live in the same dashboard.
narrative.quote: Only validated rewards deserve the habits you invest in
